title: Build NIH RePORTER Ewing extractor (deterministic, flag-on-doubt)
context: An open, source-linked map of the Ewing Sarcoma research ecosystem (organizations, grants/awards, research efforts, aggregate registered-study records, and public outputs). The first sourced slice comes from NIH RePORTER; the extractor must be deterministic, pull only from the approved channel, and flag ambiguity for human review. Binding cancer guardrails: open-access / aggregate / organization-/award-level data ONLY -- no controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA), no individual-level biobank/clinical data, no identifiable patient data; proprietary research-intelligence databases (Dimensions/WoS/Scopus/Candid) are out of scope; no medical advice or clinical interpretation. See PLAN.md.
objective: Build a deterministic NIH RePORTER extractor that pulls only the approved channel via the published inclusion-query, never infers unknown values, and records extraction method + retrievedAt into provenance.
acceptanceCriteria: Pulls only from the approved NIH RePORTER channel using the published inclusion-query.
Extraction is deterministic and assistive: maps authoritative fields; never infers unknown values; ambiguous fields are flagged for human review.
Records the extraction method and retrievedAt into every assertion's provenance.
output: extractors/nih-reporter/ extractor implementation, committed via PR.
